message:
feat(tty): replace mpsc channel with UnixStream for SIGWINCH delivery

The SIGWINCH resize notification path previously used std::sync::mpsc::
sync_channel(1) to shuttle wake signals from the signal-handler thread to
the event loop. Because mpsc channels are not file descriptors, the event
loop could not include the resize channel in its poll(2) fd set — it had
to time-slice with a 50ms ceiling to bound resize latency, trading CPU
wake-ups for responsiveness.

This commit replaces the channel with a UnixStream::pair(). The signal-
handler thread writes a single wake byte on SIGWINCH; the event loop adds
the read end to its poll(2) fd set alongside the TTY fd. Benefits:

- Zero-latency resize detection: poll(2) wakes immediately on SIGWINCH
  instead of waiting up to 50ms for the next time-slice
- No busy-loop overhead: the 50ms poll timeout ceiling is removed entirely;
  poll now blocks until either TTY input or a resize byte arrives
- Correct coalescing: drain_resize_wake_bytes() reads all pending bytes in
  a non-blocking loop, then queries the authoritative winsize via ioctl
  exactly once — identical to the old try_recv() drain but fd-native

Implementation details:
- ResizeSignalGuard::new() now takes a UnixStream (write end) instead of
  mpsc::SyncSender; the write end is set to non-blocking so the signal
  thread never blocks if the read side is slow to drain
- TtyEventSource stores resize_reader: Option<UnixStream> instead of
  resize_rx: Option<mpsc::Receiver<()>>
- drain_resize_wake_bytes() handles WouldBlock (no pending bytes),
  Interrupted (retry), and EOF/error (retire the reader permanently)
- poll_tty() builds a 1-or-2 element PollFd vec, checking revents for
  both the TTY fd and the resize fd after poll returns
- The 50ms slice_max cap in BackendEventSource::poll is removed since
  poll(2) now covers both wake sources natively

Test updates:
- resize_aware_poll_resolves_pending_escape_before_outer_timeout: swapped
  mpsc setup for UnixStream::pair()
- New test resize_wake_bytes_are_drained_and_coalesced: writes 3 wake
  bytes, verifies drain_resize_wake_bytes() returns true on first call
  and false on second (all bytes consumed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
